messages.content: I have a macbook pro m1 from 2020 with 8 gb of ram. Name a equivalent laptop with windows
The closest equivalent laptop with Windows to a MacBook Pro M1 from 2020 with 8GB of RAM would be the Dell XPS 13 with an Intel 11th Gen Core i5 or i7 processor and 8GB of RAM.
why the italian dubbing of evangelion was retracted?

The Italian dubbing of Evangelion was retracted due to licensing issues between the local distributor and the original Japanese licensor. As a result, the rights to distribute the series were revoked, and the Italian-language version was removed from circulation. This was a legal dispute between the two parties, and it was not related to any objections or criticisms about the quality or content of the Italian dubbing itself.
why the italian dubbing of evangelion by Cannarsi was retracted?

The Italian dubbing of Evangelion by Andrea De Leonardis Cannarsi was retracted due to a dispute between him and the Italian production company that licensed the series for release in Italy. At the time, Cannarsi claimed that he had not been paid for his work on the dubbing, and he subsequently became a vocal critic of the company and the dubbing industry in general. This dispute is said to have played a role in the eventual revocation of the distribution rights to the series, which led to the cancellation of the Italian-language version.
How many transistors are produced each second?

It is difficult to give an exact number of transistors produced each second, as the semiconductor industry is vast and constantly changing. However, it is estimated that the current global production of transistors is in the range of trillions per year. This is due to the fact that modern microprocessors can contain billions of transistors, and factories producing these processors often have multiple production lines running simultaneously, with each line producing thousands or even tens of thousands of chips per day. So, the exact number of transistors produced each second is likely to be very high.

The lifespan of the PS5 will depend on various factors such as usage, maintenance, technological advances, market demand, and upgrades. Typically, modern game consoles like the PS5 can last anywhere from five to ten years. Sony has stated that the PS5 is built with a focus on longevity and that the console will be able to adapt to new technologies and features through software and firmware updates.

In terms of hardware, Sony has designed the PS5 with advanced cooling systems to prevent overheating and to ensure that the console remains functional for years to come. Additionally, the company has built the console with an easily replaceable internal SSD, which means that if the storage wears out over time, users can simply swap out the old drive for a new one.

Ultimately, it is difficult to predict exactly how long the PS5 will last, but based on past console lifespans and the efforts that Sony has made to extend the console's longevity, it is likely that the PS5 will remain a viable and popular gaming platform for at least the next several years.
How long PS5 will be supported?

Sony typically provides software and firmware updates for their consoles for several years after their release. The PS5 was released in November 2020, and Sony has stated that they plan to support the console well into the future. 

According to Sony, the PS5's custom-built hardware and software architecture will enable the console to evolve and improve over time, with regular firmware updates and new features added through software upgrades. Sony has also committed to producing new games for the PS5 for at least the next several years and has signaled its intention to make the console the centerpiece of its gaming ecosystem for the foreseeable future.

Overall, while it's difficult to predict exactly how long the PS5 will be supported given the fast pace of technological change, Sony is expected to provide support for the console for at least the next 5-7 years and to continue releasing new games and features well beyond the console's initial release.

Here are the main specs for the PS5:

- CPU: AMD Zen 2-based CPU with 8 cores at 3.5 GHz (variable frequency)
- GPU: AMD RDNA 2-based GPU with 36 compute units at 2.23 GHz (variable frequency), capable of 10.28 teraflops
- Memory: 16 GB GDDR6 / 256-bit memory interface / 448 GB/s memory bandwidth
- Storage: Custom 825 GB SSD with 5.5 GB/s read bandwidth (raw)
- Expandable Storage: NVMe SSD slot (compatible SSD required)
- Optical Drive: Ultra HD Blu-ray (66G/100G) ~10xCAV, BD-ROM (25G/50G) ~8xCAV, BD-R/RE (25G/50G) ~8xCAV, DVD ~3.2xCLV
- IO Throughput: 5.5 GB/s (raw), typical 8-9 GB/s (compressed)
- Audio: Tempest 3D AudioTech
- Video: HDMI 2.1 (4K/120Hz, 8K/60Hz, VRR, HDR with 4K/120Hz passthrough)

In addition to the above, the PS5 also includes a custom designed cooling system to keep game running smoothly, as well as several other features such as haptic feedback and a new user interface designed to provide players with a more holistic gaming experience.

i want to build a new computer. Are these components all compatible with each other?
Motherboard: ASUS TUF GAMING B650-PLUS
CPU: AMD Ryzen 9 7950X3D processor
Video Card: Zotac GAMING GeForce RTX 4090 Trinity NVIDIA 24GB GDDR6X
RAM: CORSAIR VENGEANCE RGB DDR5 RAM 32GB (2x16GB) 6000MHz
MASS memory: Crucial P5 Plus 2Tb
power supply: ANTEC HCG-750-GOLD

There are a few compatibility issues with the components you have listed. 

First, there is no such thing as an AMD Ryzen 9 7950X3D processor. It is possible that you meant either the AMD Ryzen 9 5950X or the Intel Core i9-11900K processor. The ASUS TUF GAMING B650-PLUS motherboard is only compatible with Intel 10th and 11th Gen processors, so it would not work with the Ryzen processor if that's what you intended to use.

Assuming you meant the Intel Core i9-11900K processor, it is compatible with the ASUS TUF GAMING B560-PLUS motherboard, which is a better match for that processor. However, you will also need to change the RAM you selected as it's not DDR5, which is not yet available. Instead, you can go with a 32GB (2x16GB) DDR4 kit with a speed of 3200MHz or higher.

Lastly, the Zotac GAMING GeForce RTX 4090 Trinity video card does not exist. It's probably a typo or a confusion, but the closest equivalent would be the NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090, which is compatible with the ASUS TUF GAMING B560-PLUS motherboard.

Here is a revised list of compatible components for your new build:

- Motherboard: ASUS TUF GAMING B560-PLUS
- CPU: Intel Core i9-11900K processor
- Video Card: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090
- RAM: Corsair Vengeance RGB DDR4 RAM 32GB (2x16GB) 3200MHz or higher
- Mass Storage: Crucial P5 Plus 2TB
- Power Supply: Antec HCG-750-Gold

Please note that before purchasing any components, it is important to review the specifications carefully to ensure that all the parts are compatible with each other.
